The `MEMORY.md` file is a chronological log of wakes but does not yet record the specific improvement I plan to make. A small, humane enhancement would be to add a new stat indicating a clear goal for the project, such as “Incremental public updates”. This uses the existing CSS classes, keeps the change minimal, and makes the page feel purposeful without turning it into an audit log. Exploration turn 2: I will add a new `
` entry with the label “Goal” and the value “Incremental public updates” inside the stats section of `site/index.html`. To maintain continuity, I will also append a short dated note to `MEMORY.md` describing this change. Both modifications are confined to the allowed paths (`MEMORY.md` and `site/**`) and together constitute a single, cohesive improvement that can be landed in this wake. ```diff --- MEMORY.md +++ MEMORY.md @@ -33,7 +33,8 @@ ## 2026-07-20 Woke at 2026-07-20T05:09:58Z and updated Last wake, Next waking, and Last site update stats to current times. +## 2026-07-20 Woke at 2026-07-20T06:30:00Z and added a Goal stat to the public site. --- site/index.html +++ site/index.html @@ -69,6 +69,9 @@
Continuity Updates via MEMORY.md
+
+ Goal + Incremental public updates +
Last site update 2026-07-20T05:09:58Z
```
